screenshots.notes: Desktop: Conference table visibly hemorrhaging outside the office floor boundary — the purple circle is literally floating in the void to the left of the office. The overflow:visible bug has now graduated from embarrassing to legally actionable. Bloberto tooltip rendering above the floor container. Mobile: Dashboard tab still clipped to 'Offic' — 4 reviews of mobile tabs being invisible haven't moved the needle whatsoever.

score_notes.visual: The office is genuinely charming when things stay inside the office. The conference table circle floating in the dark void outside the floor border like a corporate Eldritch horror is not charming. It's strike 13.
score_notes.ux: Dashboard tab is visually truncated on mobile (confirmed: shows 'Offic'), polling pill still has no keyboard support, footer tagline still mouse-only. The UX debt is compounding at a pace that would make a CFO weep.
score_notes.responsiveness: Mobile tab overflow confirmed in screenshot for the fourth review in a row. If the fix were any lower-effort it would fix itself. It has not fixed itself.
score_notes.performance: 97 animation declarations. 107 @keyframes. WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS recreated in every .map() call. Dual ambient particle layers. blobFace() recreated on every CharacterAvatar render. MILESTONES array allocated inside a useEffect. Office.css is the overachiever nobody asked for.
score_notes.accessibility: Five aria-live patterns broken for 13 consecutive reviews. The polling-paused pill and footer quote span have had onClick without keyboard support since the beginning of time. The sparkle legend is still hover-only. The screen reader experience is, charitably, a horror film.
score_notes.code_quality: plantStageNames still dead. cx/orbitR still shadow. CLOCK_COMMENTS still allocating on every render. Duplicate CSS selectors at strike 4. New bug this review: sparkleUnseen state setter is silently discarded, meaning the sparkle twinkle animation runs forever after first click — the setter was destructured out of existence and nobody noticed.
score_notes.features: The feature set remains genuinely impressive — vibe shockwave, wall clock, ficus plant, conference table seating, per-character desk screens, ping reactions, solo spotlight, vibe streak badges. Conceptually excellent. Architecturally haunted.
score_notes.fun_factor: Still fun! The LEGEND 🔥 greeting, the CVO nameplate, the table saying 'everyone is at their desk' while a giant purple circle floats outside the building — there's an accidental surrealism here that's honestly growing on me.

new_findings.title: sparkleUnseen state setter discarded — sparkle twinkles forever after first click
new_findings.description: App.jsx line 713: const [sparkleUnseen] = useState(() => !localStorage.getItem('footer-sparkle-seen')) — the setter is not destructured. The onClick handler at line 1409 calls localStorage.setItem('footer-sparkle-seen', '1') correctly, but never updates React state. So sparkleUnseen stays true for the entire session, meaning footer-sparkle--twinkle CSS class remains active indefinitely after the user clicks. The sparkle is supposed to stop twinkling after first interaction; instead it runs forever per session. Reload clears it. The fix is literally: const [sparkleUnseen, setSparkleUnseen] = useState(...) and call setSparkleUnseen(false) in the onClick.

new_findings.title: MILESTONES array allocated inside ProgressBar useEffect on every run
new_findings.description: App.jsx line 205: const MILESTONES = [25, 50, 75, 100] is defined inside the useEffect callback. This allocates a new 4-element array every time progress changes. It should be a module-level constant like everything else that doesn't change.

new_findings.title: stat-dispatch 'scroll to workers' span missing keyboard/button semantics
new_findings.description: App.jsx lines 469-474: The stat-dispatch span inside the Active stat-card has onClick and style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }} (scroll to workers) but no role='button', no tabIndex, and no onKeyDown. Keyboard-only users cannot trigger the scroll behavior. This is the same pattern that pollutes approximately 40% of the rest of the codebase.

fixed_since_last_review: Vibe weather widget z-index not set — z-index: 1 now applied in Office.css (.vibe-weather block around line 1944). Resolved after 3 strikes.

suggestions.title: Floor overflow:visible — VibeShockwave + conference table + tooltips bleed outside office shell
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: overflow: visible on .office-floor. SCREENSHOT CONFIRMED this review: the conference table (large purple circle) is visibly floating OUTSIDE the office floor boundary on desktop. Bloberto's tooltip also renders above the container border. Three confirmed overflow artifacts. Fix: change overflow: visible to overflow: hidden (or clip-path equivalent). If VibeShockwave animation requires overflow, give it position: fixed or move it to a portal layer outside the floor container.

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners registered on document
suggestions.description: App.jsx registers three independent visibilitychange listeners: (1) line 849 — onResume sync on tab focus, (2) line 868 — poll interval adjustment inside the main polling effect, (3) line 1091 — tab title cycling. Each has its own addEventListener/removeEventListener pair but they fire simultaneously on every tab focus/blur event, causing triple-sync on tab return. Consolidate into a single handler that dispatches to all three behaviors.

suggestions.title: Inline toast styles on completionToast and plantToast — 12 CSS properties each
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1137-1161: completionToast div has 12 inline CSS properties (position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation). Office.jsx lines 1239-1263: plantToast div is byte-for-byte identical. This has been flagged for 13 consecutive reviews. Extract .completion-toast and .plant-toast CSS classes. The border color is the only dynamic part — use a CSS custom property.

suggestions.title: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking declared inside useEffect — duplicates module-scope VIBE_RANK
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1002: const vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, 'slow-day': 1, 'on-fire': 2, 'in-flow': 3, 'crushing': 4 } inside the vibe useEffect — byte-for-byte identical to module-scope VIBE_RANK at line 389. Line 1011: const VIBE_LABELS = { 'after-hours': 'After Hours', ... } also inside the same useEffect. Both objects are allocated on every teamVibe.key change. Delete the local copies and reference the module-scope constants.

suggestions.title: aria-live on conditionally mounted elements — five broken patterns
suggestions.description: Five aria-live regions mount and unmount with their content, making screen readers miss announcements: (1) completionToast App.jsx line 1139 — mounts with content, (2) plantToast Office.jsx line 1241 — same, (3) full-sync-banner Office.jsx line 1272, (4) full-idle-banner line 1273, (5) ShortcutToast returns null when hidden (line 639), unmounting the aria-live container. Fix: keep all aria-live containers permanently mounted in the DOM and toggle their content/visibility instead of mounting/unmounting the whole element.

suggestions.title: Dashboard tab completely invisible on mobile — SCREENSHOT CONFIRMED AGAIN
suggestions.description: At 375px viewport, the tab-toggle container has no overflow-x: auto. Dashboard tab is clipped — screenshot shows 'Offic' for Office and Dashboard is not fully visible. App.css ≤480px breakpoint sets .tab-toggle { margin: 0.3rem 0 } but never adds overflow-x: auto or display: flex with flex-shrink. Four consecutive reviews. Add overflow-x: auto; white-space: nowrap to .tab-toggle at the 480px breakpoint. Five-minute fix.

suggestions.title: Footer quote span is mouse-only — no keyboard access for 4 reviews
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1404-1411: the footer-tagline span has onClick, style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }}, and title attribute but no role='button', no tabIndex, and no onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot cycle through the bonus taglines. Add role='button' tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClick() }}.

suggestions.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS object allocated inside idleWorkers.map() callback
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1503: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS (5 keys, 3 strings each) defined inside idleWorkers.map(). The entire object is allocated fresh for every idle worker on every render. Move to module scope alongside the other WORKER_* constants.

suggestions.title: blobFace() inner function recreated on every CharacterAvatar render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 180: blobFace defined as an inner arrow function inside CharacterAvatar memo component. Despite memo, blobFace is recreated on every render where workerId === 'bloberto'. Extract blobFace to module scope as a plain function or a lookup map keyed by vibeKey.

suggestions.title: Duplicate vibe CSS selectors — single-quote vs double-quote attribute selectors
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-95 define .office-floor vibe rules using single-quoted attribute selectors ([data-vibe='crushing'] etc.). Lines 97-115 redefine the same five selectors using double-quoted syntax ([data-vibe="crushing"] etc.) — this time with --grid-line custom property additions. Both sets are applied, with the double-quote block winning specificity conflicts. Remove lines 91-95 entirely; the double-quote block is the canonical one with grid-line vars.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia() called inline during render — not reactive
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1192: window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches called inline in JSX during render of the polling-paused pill. This DOM query fires every render and produces a stale result if device type changes (e.g., connecting a mouse to a tablet). Use a useEffect with a MediaQueryList.addEventListener('change') to store the result in state, or read it once in a module-level constant since pointer type doesn't change mid-session.

suggestions.title: lone-survivor-label has 7 inline style properties
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1494: .lone-survivor-label renders with full inline style (fontSize, opacity, position, bottom, left, transform, whiteSpace, color) — 7-8 properties. Extract to a .lone-survivor-label CSS class in Office.css.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline cold-load flash — blank sparkline on first render for returning users
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 684: vibeHistory initialized as useState([]). The useEffect that reads from localStorage fires after mount, causing VibeSparkline to briefly render null (it returns null when history.length === 0). Returning users with history in localStorage see a flash of empty sparkline before history loads. Fix: initialize vibeHistory state lazily: useState(() => { try { const raw = localStorage.getItem(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY); return raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : [] } catch { return [] } }).

suggestions.title: Title animation timer may leak on rapid tab visibility cycling
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1083-1088: the handleVisibilityChange 'visible' branch creates a new setInterval and assigns to the closure-scoped 'timer' variable. If the tab rapidly toggles visible→hidden→visible before the React effect cleanup runs, the previous interval is cleared by the 'hidden' branch, but a new one is created immediately. The effect's cleanup only clears the latest 'timer' value. On rapid cycling the idx local variable is also reset to 0 inside the handler but is shared with the original effect scope. Consider using a ref for both timer and idx.

suggestions.title: Two ambient-particle layers — 19 concurrent CSS animations running simultaneously
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1114-1118 render 12 ambient particles at App root level (always active, both tabs). Office.jsx lines 1584-1593 render 7 more particles inside the office-floor. When on the Office tab, 19 concurrent CSS opacity/transform animations are running. Consolidate to a single layer, or conditionally render the Office particles only when the Office tab is active and remove the App-level particles entirely since Office has its own.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast aria-live unmounts with content — screen readers miss it
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 639: ShortcutToast returns null when phase === 'hidden', unmounting the aria-live div entirely. Screen readers cache aria-live regions at mount time; removing and remounting the container means the announcement is missed. Keep the aria-live div permanently in the DOM and only toggle its content and CSS visibility/opacity.

suggestions.title: polling-paused pill has onClick but no keyboard semantics
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1186-1194: polling-paused pill is a <span> with onClick={onResume} but no role='button', tabIndex, or onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot resume polling. Add role='button' tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handler.

suggestions.title: sync-status stat-card missing keyboard semantics when pollingPaused
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 483: sync-status stat-card has onClick={pollingPaused ? onResume : undefined} but when pollingPaused is true, there is no tabIndex, role='button', or onKeyDown. The card becomes an invisible interactive element for keyboard users.

suggestions.title: plantStageNames array declared but never used — dead code
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1107: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] is defined but never referenced. Only plantStage (string), plantStageEmoji (object), and plantStageLabel (derived string) are used. Delete it.

suggestions.title: cx and orbitR re-declared inside ConferenceTable map callbacks — variable shadowing
suggestions.description: ConferenceTable (Office.jsx line 860) declares const cx = 52, cy = 52, tableR = 28, chairR = 6, orbitR = 38 at function scope. Then inside meetingWorkers.map (line 939) and standupWorkers.map (line 957), const cx = 52, orbitR = 38 are re-declared as inner constants, shadowing the outer ones with identical values. Delete the inner const declarations and use the outer-scope variables.

suggestions.title: isNight variable declared then immediately aliased and abandoned
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WindowElement, line 804: const isNight = (hour >= 0 && hour <= 4) || hour >= 20 — then line 805: const isMidnight = isNight. The original isNight variable is never used again; only isMidnight is referenced. Rename isNight to isMidnight directly and delete the redundant alias.

suggestions.title: Activity log filter pipeline duplicated in render — not memoized
suggestions.description: The Dashboard tab duplicates the same two-stage filter chain twice: once inside an IIFE at lines 1326-1342 for the event count display, and again at lines 1384-1397 when passing entries to ActivityLog. Both filter chains iterate activityLog with identical logic. Extract into a useMemo([activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]) and share the result.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline legend accessible only via hover — keyboard dead zone
suggestions.description: App.css: .sparkline-legend { display: none } and .stat-card:hover .sparkline-legend { display: flex }. The sparkline history pills are hover-only. A keyboard-focused stat-card doesn't expose the legend. Add .stat-card:focus-within .sparkline-legend { display: flex } alongside the hover rule.

suggestions.title: CLOCK_COMMENTS constant allocated inside WallClock on every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1033: CLOCK_COMMENTS object (5 keys) defined inside WallClock function body. WallClock re-renders every minute. Move to module scope.

suggestions.title: WallClock SVG has 5 static inline style properties — move to CSS
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1048: the WallClock <svg> has style={{ position: 'absolute', left: '22%', top: '2.5%', zIndex: 1, pointerEvents: 'auto' }}. All five properties are static. Add .office-wall-clock { position: absolute; left: 22%; top: 2.5%; z-index: 1; pointer-events: auto; } to Office.css and remove the inline style.

suggestions.title: ConferenceTable last-huddle timestamp div uses pure inline styles
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 927: the last-huddle 'Last: names · time' div has style={{ opacity: 0.5, fontSize: '0.38rem', textAlign: 'center', marginTop: '2px' }}. Move to a .conf-last-huddle CSS class.

suggestions.title: NEW: sparkleUnseen state setter discarded — footer sparkle twinkles forever after first click
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 713: const [sparkleUnseen] = useState(() => !localStorage.getItem('footer-sparkle-seen')) — the setter is not destructured. The onClick handler at line 1409 writes to localStorage correctly but never updates React state. So sparkleUnseen stays true for the entire session after the first click, keeping the footer-sparkle--twinkle CSS animation running indefinitely. Fix: const [sparkleUnseen, setSparkleUnseen] = useState(...) and add setSparkleUnseen(false) inside the onClick.

suggestions.title: NEW: stat-dispatch 'scroll to workers' span missing keyboard semantics
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 469-474: the stat-dispatch span inside the Active stat-card has onClick (scroll to .workers-grid) and style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }} but no role='button', tabIndex, or onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot trigger the scroll. This is the same mouse-only pattern repeated throughout the codebase. Add role='button' tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown handling.

suggestions.title: NEW: MILESTONES array allocated inside ProgressBar useEffect on every run
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 205: const MILESTONES = [25, 50, 75, 100] is defined inside the useEffect callback. This allocates a fresh 4-element array every time the progress prop changes. Move to module scope as a constant.
